data.content:
The problem is clear. The target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `@(Compile)` in the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file during the build, but the generated file is never added to the `Compile` item group. Even though it runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, the file needs to be explicitly added to the `@(Compile)` items for the compiler to pick it up.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` in the target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />


</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added after the file is written, inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild's C# compiler only compiles files listed in that group, so the generated file was ignored.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target, after the file is written. This tells the compiler to include the generated source file during compilation.
